expectations.text: correct_feature_ids — All feature IDs match F-X.Y.Z format
correct_requirement_ids — All requirement IDs match R-X.Y.Z or R-X.Y.Za/b/c format
correct_story_ids — All user story IDs match US-X.Y.Z format
expectations.evidence: User story IDs use US-X.Y.Z.W format (4-level) instead of US-X.Y.Z: e.g., US-15.21.13.1, US-15.21.13.2, US-6.4.6.1, US-6.4.7.1, US-6.4.8.1, etc.

expectations.text: correct_domain — IDs use an appropriate module number for the topic
expectations.evidence: Editor features use 15.21 (Specialized Editors), runtime features use 6.4 (Haptics), both appropriate for haptic feedback authoring

expectations.text: one_to_many_requirements — At least some features have 2+ requirements
nfr_suffixes — Non-functional requirements use letter suffixes (a, b, c)
expectations.evidence: NFRs use letter suffixes: R-15.21.13a (fallback layers), R-15.21.13b (undo/redo integration), R-15.21.14a (layering), R-15.21.15a (hardware preview), R-6.4.6a (searchable), R-6.4.7a (curve remapping), R-6.4.8a (ephemeral changes)

expectations.text: one_to_many_stories — At least some features have 2+ user stories from different personas
expectations.evidence: Multiple features have 3+ stories: F-15.21.13 has 6 stories from designer (P-5), engine developer (P-26), engine tester (P-27), QA tester (P-19); F-6.4.6 has 6 stories from 4 personas

expectations.text: varied_personas — User stories reference at least 3 distinct personas
expectations.evidence: 5 distinct personas: designer (P-5), QA tester (P-19), player (P-23), engine developer (P-26), engine tester (P-27)

expectations.text: shall_in_requirements — Requirements use SHALL or SHOULD language
expectations.evidence: All 13 requirements use SHALL: e.g., R-15.21.13 'The editor SHALL provide a multi-track timeline', R-6.4.6 'The engine SHALL ship a built-in library', R-6.4.8 'The engine SHALL support live haptic parameter tuning'

expectations.text: no_shall_in_features — Feature file does not contain SHALL
expectations.evidence: features.md contains no instances of SHALL. Uses descriptive language: 'A visual editor for authoring', 'A drawable waveform canvas', 'A searchable, categorized library'

expectations.text: rationale_and_verification — Requirements include Rationale and Verification sections
expectations.evidence: All 13 requirements include Rationale and Verification subsections with specific test procedures

expectations.text: acceptance_criteria — User stories include Acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: All 33 user stories include Acceptance criteria, e.g., US-15.21.13.1: 'all five timeline tracks accept keyframes', US-6.4.8.2: 'stopping play mode without applying discards changes'

expectations.text: feature_expansions — Output includes features beyond the literal idea (suggested additions)
expectations.evidence: Features extend beyond editor authoring to include: F-6.4.7 (Haptic Parameter Binding Node — runtime logic graph integration), F-6.4.8 (Live Haptic Tuning — real-time parameter adjustment during play)

expectations.text: cross_domain_deps — Features reference dependencies on existing features from other domains
expectations.evidence: Cross-domain deps: F-15.21.13 depends on F-15.20.3 (feature editor), F-15.8.1 (logic graph runtime); F-6.4.7 depends on F-15.8.4, F-15.8.1 (logic graph); F-6.4.8 depends on F-15.1.6; F-15.21.14 depends on F-6.4.4 (audio-driven haptics)

expectations.text: user_interaction_planned — (with_skill only) user-questions.md exists with review questions
